name: Russian Zucchini Dill Casserole
recipeIngredient: 3 medium Carrots, cut into 1/4 inch dice
3 Tablespoons Chopped Fresh Parsley (plus additional for garnish)
2 Tablespoons Chopped Fresh Dill (plus additional for garnish)
3/4 Teaspoon Sugar, or more to taste
recipeInstructions:
Heavily adapted from "Vegetable-Stuffed Zucchini with Feta Cheese Sauce" in "Please to the Table -- The Russian Cookbook". It works equally well with yellow squash or bell peppers (in fact, my favorite is yellow crookneck squash), but zucchini or a mix of zucchini and squash are usually best.
1. In a large skillet or Dutch oven, sauté carrots in water or vegetable stock over medium-high heat for about 5 minutes.
2. Add onions, celery, and zucchini; continue to sauté until all vegetables are soft and colored, about 20 minutes.
3. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
4. Stir in tomato paste and sauté for 3-4 more minutes.
5. Remove from heat and stir in parsley, dill, sugar, vinegar, salt, and pepper; mix until combined.
6. Pour mixture into a flat-bottomed, heatproof casserole dish large enough to hold the ingredients.
7. Bake in the oven until zucchini are tender (about 40 minutes).
8. Sprinkle with additional chopped parsley before serving.
Optional: Add 1 cup of crumbled fat-free or fresh grated Parmesan cheese on top for a cheesy edge.
